title: Purely Crunchy Mexican Clotted Cream Huaraches
description: A fresh and crunchy Mexican dish featuring clotted cream, manteca emulsified, and no onion or garlic, served in the form of huaraches and cooked on an iron teppan.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan to medium-high heat, approximately 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the clotted cream and manteca until well combined and emulsified.
Add the shredded cheese, chopped cilantro, diced tomatoes, salt, and pepper to the bowl and stir until all the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Brush the corn tortillas with a small amount of vegetable oil on both sides to prevent sticking.
Place the tortillas on the preheated iron teppan and cook for 30 seconds on each side, until they are lightly browned and slightly puffed.
Spread a tablespoon of the clotted cream mixture onto each tortilla, leaving a small border around the edges.
Fold the tortillas in half to enclose the filling and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the cheese is melted and the tortillas are crispy.
Serve the huaraches hot, squeezed with a sliver of lime juice and garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
